sessions.title: Line Dancing Beginners
sessions.details: Hold ‘em like they do in Texas with our new line dance class. Learn a range of short, up tempo line dance routines to a range of music including Country & Western classics from the likes of Dolly Parton and Shania Twain, to current TikTok trends like Boots on the Ground and House of Bamboo. Heel tapping, thigh slapping and suitable for all experience and fitness levels, this class is all the Thursday evening fun you need to get ready for the weekend. Wear whatever is comfortable but to save our lovely dance floors, please wear jazz shoes or light soled trainers and leave your cowboy boots at the door. sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know. PLEASE NOTE: There will be no class on Thurs 12th Feb, and you will not be charged for this class when booking a term.

sessions.title: Ballet Improvers
sessions.details: Nina’s upbeat class will explore placement, spatial expansion, lyricism, and musicality. The focus will be on artistic expression, and how correct technique and coordination facilitates pace and speed. Nina began as an actress and dancer, appearing in over 60 TV, film, and stage productions before moving on to teach and coach ballet companies and dancers. Suitable if: you are 18+ and comfortable with the basics of Ballet and want to progress your technique with new challenges and combinations. We'd recommend around one year of ballet experience for this class. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know. PLEASE NOTE: There will be no class on Thurs 12th Feb, and you will not be charged for this class when booking a term.

sessions.title: Ballroom Beginners
sessions.details: Izzi blends the old elegance of Hollywood with the notion of deconstructing gender stereotypes. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. She focusses on making Ballroom and Latin dance fit for the 21st century by deconstructing the gender stereotypes and homophobia traditionally embedded within couple dancing and making the ballroom world fairer for everyone. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Professional Class
sessions.details: *Please note, from w/c 16th February, professional class will be running from Tuesday to Thursday (8:00am-9:30am). Our daily classes for professional-level dancers are led each week by a different artist from the international dance community. Each teacher brings their own style and technique to class, from their experience performing with world-leading dance companies and choreographers. With a strong focus on honing and maintaining technique, you’ll be challenged and inspired amongst peers. Professional class is ‘drop in’ and be booked by the day. You can still drop in and pay at Rambert, however as our spaces are strictly limited we recommend you book online before travelling to the building. Check out our line up for Spring 2026 below: W/C 5 January – Tristan Carter W/C 12 January – Aneta Zwierzynska W/C 19 January – Tianyi Lee (Mon) Charlotte Edmonds (Tues-Fri) W/C 26 January – Akeim Toussaint Buck W/C 2 February – Luigi Nardone W/C 9 February – Bethany Edwards W/C 16 February – Laura Vanhulle W/C 23 February – Bryony Pennington W/C 2 March – Lewis Walker W/C 9 March – Nadine Elise Muncey W/C 16 March – Yeji Kim W/C 23 March – Rosalia Panepinto Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Contemporary Beginners
sessions.details: In this contemporary class, you will be guided through the basics of contemporary technique while also exploring its Caribbean influences. Led by Rambert faculty member Paradigmz. He’s worked alongside choreographers across contemporary and classical African Peoples Dance, as well Jazz and Hip Hop and Modern Dance Forms and offers extensive experience. Artists and companies include Sakoba Dance Theatre, Jackie Guy, (Norman Rubber) Stephenson and many more. Suitable if: you’re 18+ and a beginner to contemporary dance, or maybe you’ve taken one or two classes before. This class features a live musician. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Pilates Open Level
sessions.details: This class covers most of the classical mat Pilates exercises but with a more contemporary approach and variations to train. Symeon’s teaching approach encourages you to learn how to make personal choices for your body and progress or regress according to your needs. Props are used to either challenge or simplify certain exercises. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve never taken a ballet class before, or been once or twice and want to refresh your memory. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. sessions.title: Yoga Open Level
sessions.details: These classes focus on the fine details of yoga postures and breathing, working on both your body and mind. Generally, our yoga classes start with meditation and pranayama (manipulation of the breath) and warm-up postures, followed by standing and seated asanas, and a relaxing savasana to finish. Teachers of this class come from a variety of backgrounds and will have their own individual way of teaching. Open to all levels from complete beginners to the more experienced, as the teacher adapts the class according to the students. This class is for 18+. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Peter Butler
sessions.details: A physical class consisting of a full body warm up and guided improvisation tasks followed by a short phrase incorporating the elements explored at the start of class into choreography. The aim of the class is to feel connected to your full body. Encouraging curiosity in how your breath, weight and emotion affect your physicality.

sessions.title: PHYSIO YOGA - PAY WHAT YOU CAN
sessions.details: This class is a movement meditation weaving principles of strength, motor control and proprioceptive training into intelligent yoga sequencing. Every month we will focus on a different region of the body combining sound anatomical knowledge & mindful practice to achieve efficient & graceful movement. Each vibrant flow is thoughtfully composed to allow for progressions, alternatives and modifications to promote health, wellbeing and a sense of vitality for all participants. No experience needed, all welcome.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: PILATES FLOW
sessions.details: Pilates Flow is a dynamic blend of Pilates, mobility exercises, and yoga flow sequences, all synchronized with the breath. This class aims to enhance strength, flexibility, and overall body awareness, providing a holistic workout that leaves you feeling balanced and revitalized.

sessions.title: YOGA for over 50s
sessions.details: Classes involve simple sequences of movements aiming at building strength and balance while keeping you connected to breath and intention.

sessions.title: VINYASA
sessions.details: Power Vinyasa Yoga is a vigorous, strong approach to the vinyasa yoga practice. Expect a full-body strong flow, arm balances, conditioning exercises as well as deep openers.

sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Inspired on rocket yoga, sequences are energising, inspiring, sometimes hilarious – and with optional balances & inversions to get the heart pumping. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

sessions.title: CONTEMPORARY DANCE
sessions.details: Nicola’s teaching philosophy centers on fostering an inclusive, safe space for students to explore their creativity and connect with others. She emphasizes presence, adaptability, and self-expression while encouraging students to embrace challenges and build confidence in their artistry. Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW 90
sessions.details: Ann has been teaching a soft, creative style of Vinyasa Flow across London for over 10 years. Her original teacher training was 500 hours BWY with Danny DeVille. She has since sought out wisdom from many wonderful teachers- including Jason Crandell adding texture and depth to this foundation. Her interest in how the body moves is largely influenced by her work in dance, whereas the breathing, asana and esoteric elements stem from her education in Yoga and its rich history of ideas and styles.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. sessions.title: COMMUNITY VINYASA FLOW with YTT course Graduates
sessions.details: Join our newly graduate teachers for an hour of creative and intelligently sequenced Vinyasa Flow. Hot from the graduation press, our 2025 Yoga Teacher Training cohort will hold space in rotation to practice their teaching skills and launch their careers. Each teacher will share their unique teaching style patiently and excitingly developed throughout our course under the trusty guide of lead teachers Ann Pidcock and Roberto Rubalcava. Classes will be devised to be layered and accessible: all are welcome, plenty of modifications will be offered. Come and meet this new generation of lovely and creative teachers! Classes are £5.

sessions.title: The Expressive Body Workshop
sessions.details: Laura Workshop is inspired by the body’s Chakra points, using them to initiate acrobatic and emotive movement. The session focuses on the connection between the head, spine, and tailbone, creating a serpentine flow that enhances movement fluidity. Her approach explores the contrast between gathering and sending, high and low, as well as emotionally driven movement and functional movement patterns. Drawing from 30 years of experience in dance and a growing curiosity in martial arts, Laura integrates a highly physical and expressive movement language into her teaching. Her practice challenges dancers to embrace both technical precision and emotional depth, fostering a dynamic and immersive experience.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: PREGNANCY VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: This class is an active yoga practice for mums-to-be throughout their second and third trimesters. The poses are safely adapted to encourage strength, flexibility, balance and ease. The class has a steady pace with plenty of options for all levels of experience and also includes breathing techniques and deep relaxation, both of which are so vital to support and nourish the mother and growing baby. *Please note that you need to be 13 weeks pregnant to begin the classes.

sessions.title: AMBIENT DANCE
sessions.details: How can we nourish our creative seeds as dancers and artists? Combining contact improvisation, journaling, floorwork and butoh-inspired scores, this 2-hour workshop offers space to re-focus and develop your creative ideas. Led by choreographer and dance theatremaker Billy Maxwell Taylor, you will meet a quiet humming energy throughout, both giving the body care during Spring and offering vital space to develop your dreams. Billy calls this the cultivation of tranquility and vitality. Ambient Dance is not a flashy technique, but a soft philosophy that allows us to meet the dance of life with care and warmth. The turning of leaves. The sparkling of city lights. The tidying of our homes. The expansion of our lungs. Whether you are a dancer, a physical theatre performer, a choreographer, a yoga practitioner or an artist curious in movement, this session will facilitate your experience with deep listening and consideration. We'll also have some deliciously brewed coffee and biscuits.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. sessions.title: YIN & SOUND
sessions.details: Yin yoga uses long-held relaxed floor postures to stimulate, stretch and nourish body and mind - in particular, the deep, connective tissues of the body. It is a quiet, yet profound practice and derives from theories contained in Chinese medicine and Taoism. These theories have parallels with other yoga traditions. The aim of yin yoga is to create a free-flow of energy (‘chi’ or, in Indian yoga tradition, ‘prana’) through the body. The body’s deep tissues carry most of our fluid systems and by stimulating them and creating spaciousness around them with sustained yoga postures we improve the function of all our fundamental systems. Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. Yin practice focuses on ‘stillness’ in holding poses to achieve these physical benefits but also invites the mind to ease and tune-in with deeper awareness to the subtle shifts of energy experienced as the body tissues release. This can be a very rewarding and energizing process! * in order to receive the full benefits of the sound healing, we recommend attending this class in person as we cannot guarantee the quality of the sound online

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. sessions.title: ProDance class with Sophie Ormiston
sessions.details: Sophie's practise and movement language places focus on the articulation and availability of the entirety of the spine to create a sense of fluidity within the body. Together we will research ‘importance’ within movement through improvisation and some set phrases. We will explore how we can make the small and mundane important through our commitment to movement by noticing and harnessing sensation. Whilst using these elements as a way into soft, fluid full bodied physicality we will contrastingly explore how we can make dance less serious, silly and somewhat unimportant. Through the use of abstract visual references and tasks we will find ways into different dynamics and qualities that not only involve the body but also the face and voice. Each day the structure and content of my class will change with some things remaining consistent. Often including meditation, improvisation, phrases, crossings as well as voice and breathwork.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset.

sessions.title: VINYASA
sessions.details: This Vinyasa Yoga class offers a dynamic, breath-led practice that builds strength, mobility and ease in the body. Movement flows smoothly from one posture to the next, linking breath with mindful transitions to create a continuous, energising sequence. Expect a balanced mix of standing postures, core work, creative sequencing and grounding stretches, with options offered to support different levels of experience. Classes may vary in pace and intensity, inviting both challenge and softness, while encouraging awareness, presence and connection. Suitable for those with some experience of yoga, as well as confident beginners looking to explore a flowing practice, this class leaves you feeling centred, uplifted and reconnected, both on and off the mat.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Sophie Ormiston
sessions.details: Sophie's practise and movement language places focus on the articulation and availability of the entirety of the spine to create a sense of fluidity within the body. Together we will research ‘importance’ within movement through improvisation and some set phrases. We will explore how we can make the small and mundane important through our commitment to movement by noticing and harnessing sensation. Whilst using these elements as a way into soft, fluid full bodied physicality we will contrastingly explore how we can make dance less serious, silly and somewhat unimportant. Through the use of abstract visual references and tasks we will find ways into different dynamics and qualities that not only involve the body but also the face and voice. Each day the structure and content of my class will change with some things remaining consistent. Often including meditation, improvisation, phrases, crossings as well as voice and breathwork.

sessions.title: BAMBU BODIES
sessions.details: Bambu Bodies is a mixed movement class inspired by the Capoeira practice. The class begins with a set sequence to help with conditioning before moving onto movement inquiries involving anything from floorwork and rhythm, to partner-work, to proprioceptive tasks and practices, developing strength, mobility, and co-ordination on the way.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

sessions.title: YIN & MEDITATION
sessions.details: Yin yoga uses long-held relaxed floor postures to stimulate, stretch and nourish body and mind - in particular, the deep, connective tissues of the body. It is a quiet, yet profound practice and derives from theories contained in Chinese medicine and Taoism. These theories have parallels with other yoga traditions. The aim of yin yoga is to create a free-flow of energy (‘chi’ or, in Indian yoga tradition, ‘prana’) through the body. The body’s deep tissues carry most of our fluid systems and by stimulating them and creating spaciousness around them with sustained yoga postures we improve the function of all our fundamental systems. Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. Yin practice focuses on ‘stillness’ in holding poses to achieve these physical benefits but also invites the mind to ease and tune-in with deeper awareness to the subtle shifts of energy experienced as the body tissues release. This can be a very rewarding and energizing process!

sessions.title: PILATES FLOW
sessions.details: Pilates Flow is a dynamic blend of Pilates, mobility exercises, and yoga flow sequences, all synchronized with the breath. This class aims to enhance strength, flexibility, and overall body awareness, providing a holistic workout that leaves you feeling balanced and revitalized.

sessions.title: YOGA for over 50s
sessions.details: Classes involve simple sequences of movements aiming at building strength and balance while keeping you connected to breath and intention.

sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Inspired on rocket yoga, sequences are energising, inspiring, sometimes hilarious – and with optional balances & inversions to get the heart pumping. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

sessions.title: CONTEMPORARY DANCE
sessions.details: Nicola’s teaching philosophy centers on fostering an inclusive, safe space for students to explore their creativity and connect with others. She emphasizes presence, adaptability, and self-expression while encouraging students to embrace challenges and build confidence in their artistry. Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW 90
sessions.details: Ann has been teaching a soft, creative style of Vinyasa Flow across London for over 10 years. Her original teacher training was 500 hours BWY with Danny DeVille. She has since sought out wisdom from many wonderful teachers- including Jason Crandell adding texture and depth to this foundation. Her interest in how the body moves is largely influenced by her work in dance, whereas the breathing, asana and esoteric elements stem from her education in Yoga and its rich history of ideas and styles.

sessions.title: Spring Equinox: Exploring Equanimity
sessions.details: The Spring Equinox in the northern hemisphere signals the balance between day and night - leaving the winter behind and move fully towards spring. The practice will explore how we find balance while letting go of the past and planting seeds for the future. The workshop will weave meditation, yoga, writing/creative expression and sound as tools for to navigate between endings and new beginnings *** £25 Early Bird price before 14th March

sessions.title: PREGNANCY VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: This class is an active yoga practice for mums-to-be throughout their second and third trimesters. The poses are safely adapted to encourage strength, flexibility, balance and ease. The class has a steady pace with plenty of options for all levels of experience and also includes breathing techniques and deep relaxation, both of which are so vital to support and nourish the mother and growing baby. *Please note that you need to be 13 weeks pregnant to begin the classes.

sessions.title: Couples Massage Workshop
sessions.details: Massage is one of the most heartfelt ways to show care, a simple, meaningful way to connect through touch. Join us for a two-hour Couple’s Massage Workshop, designed for partners, friends, or family members who’d like to share a moment of relaxation and reconnection. You’ll learn gentle, soothing techniques, how to create a calming atmosphere, and ways to communicate through mindful touch for a deeply restorative experience. Fully clothed and performed on the floor, this practice is easy to recreate at home — with optional oils for extra comfort. The session is pregnancy-friendly and suitable for all levels. **Please note: this workshop is for personal enrichment and does not provide professional certification. One ticket per person is required.

sessions.title: FREE YOGA TEACHER TRAINING '26 - Q&A SESSION
sessions.details: Curious about our Yoga Teacher Training course? Ready to explore the ever-evolving, beautifully layered tradition of yoga? Want to deepen your understanding and refine your practice? If you’re hesitating—or simply want to learn more about our YTT—join TripSpace founder Giuliana Majo and lead teachers Ann Pidcock and Roberto Rubalcava for a FREE Q&A session, including a complimentary in-person class. This is your chance to ask questions, feel the energy, and dip your toes into this transformative journey. At the end, you’ll have the opportunity to book a FREE class with one of our lead teachers and experience the vibes firsthand.

sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: YOGA for over 50s
sessions.details: Classes involve simple sequences of movements aiming at building strength and balance while keeping you connected to breath and intention.

sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Inspired on rocket yoga, sequences are energising, inspiring, sometimes hilarious – and with optional balances & inversions to get the heart pumping. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

sessions.title: CONTEMPORARY DANCE
sessions.details: Nicola’s teaching philosophy centers on fostering an inclusive, safe space for students to explore their creativity and connect with others. She emphasizes presence, adaptability, and self-expression while encouraging students to embrace challenges and build confidence in their artistry. Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW 90
sessions.details: Ann has been teaching a soft, creative style of Vinyasa Flow across London for over 10 years. Her original teacher training was 500 hours BWY with Danny DeVille. She has since sought out wisdom from many wonderful teachers- including Jason Crandell adding texture and depth to this foundation. Her interest in how the body moves is largely influenced by her work in dance, whereas the breathing, asana and esoteric elements stem from her education in Yoga and its rich history of ideas and styles.

sessions.title: PREGNANCY VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: This class is an active yoga practice for mums-to-be throughout their second and third trimesters. The poses are safely adapted to encourage strength, flexibility, balance and ease. The class has a steady pace with plenty of options for all levels of experience and also includes breathing techniques and deep relaxation, both of which are so vital to support and nourish the mother and growing baby. *Please note that you need to be 13 weeks pregnant to begin the classes.

sessions.title: Flying Low
sessions.details: Developed by David Zambrano, Flying Low is a floorwork technique that teaches smooth transitions into and out of the floor using spirals, sliding, and fluid sequences. It builds the foundation for effortless connection between the body and its surroundings—integral to the art of Passing Through.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: YIN & SOUND
sessions.details: Yin yoga uses long-held relaxed floor postures to stimulate, stretch and nourish body and mind - in particular, the deep, connective tissues of the body. It is a quiet, yet profound practice and derives from theories contained in Chinese medicine and Taoism. These theories have parallels with other yoga traditions. The aim of yin yoga is to create a free-flow of energy (‘chi’ or, in Indian yoga tradition, ‘prana’) through the body. The body’s deep tissues carry most of our fluid systems and by stimulating them and creating spaciousness around them with sustained yoga postures we improve the function of all our fundamental systems. Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. Yin practice focuses on ‘stillness’ in holding poses to achieve these physical benefits but also invites the mind to ease and tune-in with deeper awareness to the subtle shifts of energy experienced as the body tissues release. This can be a very rewarding and energizing process! * in order to receive the full benefits of the sound healing, we recommend attending this class in person as we cannot guarantee the quality of the sound online

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset.

sessions.title: VINYASA
sessions.details: This Vinyasa Yoga class offers a dynamic, breath-led practice that builds strength, mobility and ease in the body. Movement flows smoothly from one posture to the next, linking breath with mindful transitions to create a continuous, energising sequence. Expect a balanced mix of standing postures, core work, creative sequencing and grounding stretches, with options offered to support different levels of experience. Classes may vary in pace and intensity, inviting both challenge and softness, while encouraging awareness, presence and connection. Suitable for those with some experience of yoga, as well as confident beginners looking to explore a flowing practice, this class leaves you feeling centred, uplifted and reconnected, both on and off the mat.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: PILATES FLOW
sessions.details: Pilates Flow is a dynamic blend of Pilates, mobility exercises, and yoga flow sequences, all synchronized with the breath. This class aims to enhance strength, flexibility, and overall body awareness, providing a holistic workout that leaves you feeling balanced and revitalized.

sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Inspired on rocket yoga, sequences are energising, inspiring, sometimes hilarious – and with optional balances & inversions to get the heart pumping. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

sessions.title: CONTEMPORARY DANCE
sessions.details: Nicola’s teaching philosophy centers on fostering an inclusive, safe space for students to explore their creativity and connect with others. She emphasizes presence, adaptability, and self-expression while encouraging students to embrace challenges and build confidence in their artistry. Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW 90
sessions.details: Ann has been teaching a soft, creative style of Vinyasa Flow across London for over 10 years. Her original teacher training was 500 hours BWY with Danny DeVille. She has since sought out wisdom from many wonderful teachers- including Jason Crandell adding texture and depth to this foundation. Her interest in how the body moves is largely influenced by her work in dance, whereas the breathing, asana and esoteric elements stem from her education in Yoga and its rich history of ideas and styles.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: YIN MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: PREGNANCY VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: This class is an active yoga practice for mums-to-be throughout their second and third trimesters. The poses are safely adapted to encourage strength, flexibility, balance and ease. The class has a steady pace with plenty of options for all levels of experience and also includes breathing techniques and deep relaxation, both of which are so vital to support and nourish the mother and growing baby. *Please note that you need to be 13 weeks pregnant to begin the classes.

sessions.title: Gaga People
sessions.details: Gaga is the movement language developed by Ohad Naharin throughout many years, parallel to his work as a choreographer and artistic director (1990-2018) of Batsheva Dance Company. Gaga originated from Naharin’s need to communicate with his dancers and his curiosity in the ongoing research of movement. Gaga classes are predicated on a deep listening to the body and to physical sensations. The instructions are deployed to increase awareness of and further amplify sensation, and rather than turning from one prompt to another, information is layered, building into a multisensory, physically challenging experience. While many instructions are imbued with rich imagery, the research of Gaga is fundamentally physical, insisting on a specific process of embodiment. Inside this shared research, the improvisational nature of the exploration enables each participant’s deeply personal connection with Gaga. Throughout the class, participants are guided by a series of evocative instructions deployed to increase awareness of and further amplify sensation. Rather than turning from one prompt to another, information is layered, building into a multisensory, physically challenging experience. While many instructions are imbued with rich imagery, the research of Gaga is fundamentally physical, insisting on a specific process of embodiment. Inside this shared research, the improvisational nature of the exploration enables each participant’s deeply personal connection with Gaga. Participants should wear comfortable clothes and be prepared to dance barefoot or in socks. It is advisable to bring a bottle of water and a towel for use after class.

Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: YIN & SOUND
sessions.details: Yin yoga uses long-held relaxed floor postures to stimulate, stretch and nourish body and mind - in particular, the deep, connective tissues of the body. It is a quiet, yet profound practice and derives from theories contained in Chinese medicine and Taoism. These theories have parallels with other yoga traditions. The aim of yin yoga is to create a free-flow of energy (‘chi’ or, in Indian yoga tradition, ‘prana’) through the body. The body’s deep tissues carry most of our fluid systems and by stimulating them and creating spaciousness around them with sustained yoga postures we improve the function of all our fundamental systems. Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. Yin practice focuses on ‘stillness’ in holding poses to achieve these physical benefits but also invites the mind to ease and tune-in with deeper awareness to the subtle shifts of energy experienced as the body tissues release. This can be a very rewarding and energizing process! * in order to receive the full benefits of the sound healing, we recommend attending this class in person as we cannot guarantee the quality of the sound online

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

sessions.title: ProDance class with Miles Kearley
sessions.details: Miles uses the umbrella term of contemporary dance to blend his own style with different movement practices. This pro class starts with a series of effective mobility and mindful exercises. Through different tasks we will explore and research movement qualities from free-flowing, undulating, multi-directional to dynamic, rhythmic and explosive. We will use the floor, each other and set phrases to investigate different movement mechanics and energetic states. Our explorations will be applied to a final task, where we can enjoy the feeling of moving collectively in space. The elements of musicality, play and synergy are explored throughout.

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset.

sessions.title: VINYASA
sessions.details: This Vinyasa Yoga class offers a dynamic, breath-led practice that builds strength, mobility and ease in the body. Movement flows smoothly from one posture to the next, linking breath with mindful transitions to create a continuous, energising sequence. Expect a balanced mix of standing postures, core work, creative sequencing and grounding stretches, with options offered to support different levels of experience. Classes may vary in pace and intensity, inviting both challenge and softness, while encouraging awareness, presence and connection. Suitable for those with some experience of yoga, as well as confident beginners looking to explore a flowing practice, this class leaves you feeling centred, uplifted and reconnected, both on and off the mat.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

sessions.details: A slow-paced yet strong yoga practice that merges the longer postures of Hatha with the love for creative sequencing and breath of vinyasa (Anusara inspired), injecting philosophy themes, mindfulness, pranayama and mantras. To remind us that the space of Yoga is a supportive container. One that cultivates awareness, embodied strength, and a steady return to the present moment.

sessions.title: LATIN-FUSION DANCE
sessions.details: A joyful, high-energy class blending Latin dance styles with contemporary movement and expressive flow. In this 60-minute session, you’ll explore rhythm, musicality, and full-body movement through accessible choreography and playful combinations. Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

sessions.title: PHYSIO YOGA - PAY WHAT YOU CAN
sessions.details: This class is a movement meditation weaving principles of strength, motor control and proprioceptive training into intelligent yoga sequencing. Every month we will focus on a different region of the body combining sound anatomical knowledge & mindful practice to achieve efficient & graceful movement. Each vibrant flow is thoughtfully composed to allow for progressions, alternatives and modifications to promote health, wellbeing and a sense of vitality for all participants. No experience needed, all welcome.

Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

sessions.title: YIN & MEDITATION
sessions.details: Yin yoga uses long-held relaxed floor postures to stimulate, stretch and nourish body and mind - in particular, the deep, connective tissues of the body. It is a quiet, yet profound practice and derives from theories contained in Chinese medicine and Taoism. These theories have parallels with other yoga traditions. The aim of yin yoga is to create a free-flow of energy (‘chi’ or, in Indian yoga tradition, ‘prana’) through the body. The body’s deep tissues carry most of our fluid systems and by stimulating them and creating spaciousness around them with sustained yoga postures we improve the function of all our fundamental systems. Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. Yin practice focuses on ‘stillness’ in holding poses to achieve these physical benefits but also invites the mind to ease and tune-in with deeper awareness to the subtle shifts of energy experienced as the body tissues release. This can be a very rewarding and energizing process!

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: HATHA FLOW
sessions.details: A slow-paced yet strong yoga practice that merges the longer postures of Hatha with the love for creative sequencing and breath of vinyasa (Anusara inspired), injecting philosophy themes, mindfulness, pranayama and mantras. To remind us that the space of Yoga is a supportive container. One that cultivates awareness, embodied strength, and a steady return to the present moment.

sessions.title: LATIN-FUSION DANCE
sessions.details: A joyful, high-energy class blending Latin dance styles with contemporary movement and expressive flow. In this 60-minute session, you’ll explore rhythm, musicality, and full-body movement through accessible choreography and playful combinations. Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

sessions.title: PHYSIO YOGA - PAY WHAT YOU CAN
sessions.details: This class is a movement meditation weaving principles of strength, motor control and proprioceptive training into intelligent yoga sequencing. Every month we will focus on a different region of the body combining sound anatomical knowledge & mindful practice to achieve efficient & graceful movement. Each vibrant flow is thoughtfully composed to allow for progressions, alternatives and modifications to promote health, wellbeing and a sense of vitality for all participants. No experience needed, all welcome.

sessions.title: PILATES FLOW
sessions.details: Pilates Flow is a dynamic blend of Pilates, mobility exercises, and yoga flow sequences, all synchronized with the breath. This class aims to enhance strength, flexibility, and overall body awareness, providing a holistic workout that leaves you feeling balanced and revitalized.

sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Inspired on rocket yoga, sequences are energising, inspiring, sometimes hilarious – and with optional balances & inversions to get the heart pumping. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

sessions.title: CONTEMPORARY DANCE
sessions.details: Nicola’s teaching philosophy centers on fostering an inclusive, safe space for students to explore their creativity and connect with others. She emphasizes presence, adaptability, and self-expression while encouraging students to embrace challenges and build confidence in their artistry. Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are linked together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath.   Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW 90
sessions.details: Ann has been teaching a soft, creative style of Vinyasa Flow across London for over 10 years. Her original teacher training was 500 hours BWY with Danny DeVille. She has since sought out wisdom from many wonderful teachers- including Jason Crandell adding texture and depth to this foundation. Her interest in how the body moves is largely influenced by her work in dance, whereas the breathing, asana and esoteric elements stem from her education in Yoga and its rich history of ideas and styles.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. Benefits include cardiovascular health, strength building, a wide range of motion, stress relief, flexibility and connection with breath. Expect to move from pose to pose in a creative sequence that changes every time, and often with a musical background. Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: YIN MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: Improvisation into Choreography Workshop
sessions.details: This workshop is designed for those who wish to deepen and challenge their dance practice within a playful and reflective environment. Drawing from over a decade of developing her improvisational and choreographic practice, Faye invites participants to explore movement through curiosity, offering puzzles and challenges for both body and mind that cultivate complexity, receptiveness, control, spontaneity and anatomical awareness. Following a gentle mental and physical warm-up, Faye guides the group through a series of improvisation tasks, with moments for reflection and processing throughout the workshop through dialogue, drawing and writing. She will also share choreographed material from her works, creating an opportunity to explore how improvisation can shape and enrich the experience of dancing set material. photo credit: Kirsten McTernan.

sessions.title: PREGNANCY VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: This class is an active yoga practice for mums-to-be throughout their second and third trimesters. The poses are safely adapted to encourage strength, flexibility, balance and ease. The class has a steady pace with plenty of options for all levels of experience and also includes breathing techniques and deep relaxation, both of which are so vital to support and nourish the mother and growing baby. *Please note that you need to be 13 weeks pregnant to begin the classes.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Bryony Pennington
sessions.details: This professional level contemporary class is rooted in a loose Cunningham style structure, where technique should feel familiar and clean. From there we layer, seeking release within structure and expanding into the space. Gradually testing the physical limits with each exercise. We will play with risk, momentum and weight, building trust in your centre of gravity so you can move bigger and more confidently while staying precise.

sessions.title: VINYASA
sessions.details: This Vinyasa Yoga class offers a dynamic, breath-led practice that builds strength, mobility and ease in the body. Movement flows smoothly from one posture to the next, linking breath with mindful transitions to create a continuous, energising sequence. Expect a balanced mix of standing postures, core work, creative sequencing and grounding stretches, with options offered to support different levels of experience. Classes may vary in pace and intensity, inviting both challenge and softness, while encouraging awareness, presence and connection. Suitable for those with some experience of yoga, as well as confident beginners looking to explore a flowing practice, this class leaves you feeling centred, uplifted and reconnected, both on and off the mat.

Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Inspired on rocket yoga, sequences are energising, inspiring, sometimes hilarious – and with optional balances & inversions to get the heart pumping. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

sessions.title: CONTEMPORARY DANCE
sessions.details: Nicola’s teaching philosophy centers on fostering an inclusive, safe space for students to explore their creativity and connect with others. She emphasizes presence, adaptability, and self-expression while encouraging students to embrace challenges and build confidence in their artistry. Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW

Some classes will be more vigorous and challenging than others depending on the teacher, who each bring their own influences, so do try them all or contact us for guidance.

sessions.title: VINYASA FLOW 90
sessions.details: Ann has been teaching a soft, creative style of Vinyasa Flow across London for over 10 years. Her original teacher training was 500 hours BWY with Danny DeVille. She has since sought out wisdom from many wonderful teachers- including Jason Crandell adding texture and depth to this foundation. Her interest in how the body moves is largely influenced by her work in dance, whereas the breathing, asana and esoteric elements stem from her education in Yoga and its rich history of ideas and styles.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA
sessions.details: Dynamic Vinyasa flow yoga is a dynamic practice rooted in Ashtanga yoga principles, where poses are link together to form one fluid sequence of movement. sessions.title: YIN MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: FLEXIBILITY/BACKBENDS WORKSHOP
sessions.details: This workshop is designed for yoga practitioners who would like to improve their flexibility in a playful, supportive environment. During the workshop, students will be guided through a sequence of postures that progressively build towards deeper “backbends”. Nadia uses quotation marks because flexibility is truly a full-body practice; the spine itself is less flexible than many people think. Along the way, Nadia will introduce the core principles of Prana Vashya flexibility and alignment so students understand the role each posture plays in the sequence, including the less glamorous ones. In her own practice, the greatest progress has come from consistently returning to these fundamentals. Cobra is her best friend, and hopefully it will become yours too by the end of the workshop. Within the group setting, Nadia will also offer individual guidance to support each participant in working towards their own flexibility goals.

sessions.title: PREGNANCY VINYASA FLOW
sessions.details: This class is an active yoga practice for mums-to-be throughout their second and third trimesters. The poses are safely adapted to encourage strength, flexibility, balance and ease. The class has a steady pace with plenty of options for all levels of experience and also includes breathing techniques and deep relaxation, both of which are so vital to support and nourish the mother and growing baby. *Please note that you need to be 13 weeks pregnant to begin the classes.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with OzzyBerzin-Cohen
sessions.details: Sweat, Strength and Totality - the journey of class. Through diving into sensation and groove, we will explore story telling as we find humour, character and individuality through improvisational research. Class with explore a range of textures and images as we move through guided improvisation, engaging the core challenging the body. Through crossings and set phrases we will explore the totality of movement, from the smallest gesture to the largest groove, as we move through character and humour driven phrases. This is a space for fun and accessible play, to research and laugh together.

sessions.title: DYNAMIC VINYASA

sessions.title: YIN MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: YIN & SOUND
sessions.details: Yin yoga uses long-held relaxed floor postures to stimulate, stretch and nourish body and mind - in particular, the deep, connective tissues of the body. It is a quiet, yet profound practice and derives from theories contained in Chinese medicine and Taoism. These theories have parallels with other yoga traditions. The aim of yin yoga is to create a free-flow of energy (‘chi’ or, in Indian yoga tradition, ‘prana’) through the body. The body’s deep tissues carry most of our fluid systems and by stimulating them and creating spaciousness around them with sustained yoga postures we improve the function of all our fundamental systems. Gradual and sustained lengthening throughout the body's joints and tissues improves the circulation, respiration, detoxification and benefits both mobility and agility. Yin practice focuses on ‘stillness’ in holding poses to achieve these physical benefits but also invites the mind to ease and tune-in with deeper awareness to the subtle shifts of energy experienced as the body tissues release. This can be a very rewarding and energizing process! * in order to receive the full benefits of the sound healing, we recommend attending this class in person as we cannot guarantee the quality of the sound online

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Orla Hardie
sessions.details: Orla’s teaching practice is an exploration of the imagination, using texture and self-caricature as pathways to connect to movement. Supported by an array of music genres and guided improvisation, we will move through a range of dynamics from gooey totality to squeaky articulation. Moving through set material, we’ll dissolve in and out of the floor, getting down, sweaty, and fully immersed. Together we create a space where we can dive into the mind, liberate the body, and play.

sessions.title: FLOW & GONG
sessions.details: The ultimate way to flow, relax, and glow. First stretch out and strengthen up with a dynamic vinyasa practice that links breath to movement, and really gets you moving. Next the pace drops as you move into deeper, more restorative poses and calming breathwork – before full sensory submersion in a 30-minute gong bath. Lie back and let the healing vibrations lead you into a state of contemplation, relaxation, and positivity. You'll leave glowing for sure!

Through her classes, Nicola seeks to inspire boldness, community, and joy in movement. This all-level class invites participants to explore movement improvisation, flow, and groove. Structured exercises and physical sequences focus on bold, dynamic choices and artistry, blending elements of soft acrobatics, floorwork, classical dance techniques, and yoga.
