sessions.title: PHYSIO YOGA - PAY WHAT YOU CAN
sessions.details: This class is a movement meditation weaving principles of strength, motor control and proprioceptive training into intelligent yoga sequencing. Every month we will focus on a different region of the body combining sound anatomical knowledge & mindful practice to achieve efficient & graceful movement. Each vibrant flow is thoughtfully composed to allow for progressions, alternatives and modifications to promote health, wellbeing and a sense of vitality for all participants. No experience needed, all welcome.

sessions.title: Monday Beginners Level 1 (Course of 6 classes)
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays 05/01, 12/01, 19/01, 26/01, 02/02 and 09/02 from 6:30 to 8:30pm. Classes taught by Rick Nodine. The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) We will explore creativity and improvisation so that we can maintain joy and curiosity in our dancing 6) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sqhh-20260105183000

sessions.title: Saturday CI Workshop: Jam Skills
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • This workshop is about how to get more out of Jamming. CI Jams are exciting spaces open to possibilities, but how do we access those possibilities? We will work with strategies for getting in and out of dances, enjoying being lost and look at what it means to be available for the unexpected to happen. We will unpack some jamming etiquette and how to avoid being a ‘jerk’ in a jam. There will be structured improvisation exercises that will explore jam scenarios and plenty of jamming with prompts to help you expand your jamming persona. As always with my workshops, you can choose your own level of challenge. Technical study will evolve into free Contact Dancing, where we can explore and integrate new learnings into improvisation. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sqtf-20260207143000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation Intensive With Rick Nodine: Training Ground, Winter Edition
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • London, Thursday February 19th to Sunday February 22nd, 10h-17h In this intensive 4 days of study we will explore the ground that CI dances on. We will build our connect to earth, through the floor, with solo Somatics and simple but essential patterns that underpin the CI body. We will grow confidence in falling, rolling and inverting ourselves so that our partner dances can expand beyond habitual limits. Extended contact dancing guided by scores that demand connection through principles will guide us to the edges we need to work at. We will also reclaim the floor as playground for fresh explorations of the form and the pleasures of improvisation for its own sake. Time to explore, space to discover and days to build new skills will lead to questions and some answers about the dances you are looking for. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sbe5w-20260219100000

sessions.title: Bound & Unbound Weekend, Spring Edition: Exploring the Edges of Contact Improvisation
sessions.details: Anatomie Studio, Arch 113 Unit 17 Station Passage, London, SE15 2JR • A three day intensive with Jamus Wood & Rick Nodine. Contact Improvisation, Shibari & BMC®. In this short Intensive we will take time to explore some of the territory at the edges of Contact Improvisation. Exploring aspects of the dance that often go unrecognised or don’t receive quality attention. Contact improvisation, led by Rick, will be at the heart of our practice and the ground of our shared language. We will spend the first part of our time together dropping into inner and outer awareness through Body Mind Centering® exercises and perspectives. The intention is to bring a felt sense of embodiment and a deep listening to our dance. Later we will draw inspiration from Shibari (Japanese Rope Play) with Jamus. Initially, we’ll focus on the sensations of our bodies in relation to one another, without ropes yet through physical limitations, exploring binding and unbinding, being held and being free. We will then introduce ropes, engaging in Ichinawa (one rope technique) to explore further the qualities of resistance and surrender, stillness and breath, gravity and flow. A key aspect of our workshop will be The Switch —a playful exploration of roles as we transition between being bound and binding with one’s body. We will slow down and zoom in, lingering in each role for some time. Also taking moments when neither of us are in a role. We will emphasise consent throughout our time together. Consent will be taught as a practice so that we can dance within our limits while finding and exploring our edges together. We’ll conclude each day with an exploratory jam, allowing space to integrate what we've explored and to express ourselves freely, weaving our individual journeys into a collective tapestry of creativity and joy. Join us in this supportive circle of explorers. Together, let’s celebrate the borders of Contact Improvisation with Shibari, deepening our connections through movement, trust and shared discovery. Limited tickets available https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-stnbu-20260327183000

The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sis0w-20260427183000

sessions.title: Summer CI retreat with Rick Nodine, Tender Agility, Gaia Terra
sessions.details: Gaia Terra, Via Francesco Petrarca, 45, Rivignano Teor, UD, 33061 • It is called Tender Agility, and it's all in the name: tender: /ˈtɛndə/ 1. showing kindness and affection agility: /əˈdʒɪlɪti/ 1. ability to move quickly and easily It will be a 6 day CI intensive (24-30 August 2026) in the North of Italy, with workshops, bodywork and somatics sessions, Jams, hang out in the sun and swim in the river. We have been wanting to collaborate with Gaia Terra for a long time, so we are excited about spending a week in this Ecovillage nested in beautiful countryside, equipped with a state-of-the-art dance studio and gardens where they grow the food we will eat. CI Workshops will include: Somatic exploration CI technique/patterns and skills Exploration space Where? Gaia Terra, in the North of Italy. Gaia Terra is an Ecovillage nested in beautiful nature equipped with a state-of-the-art dance studio, beautiful surroundings and a river! Gaia Terra is more than just a site that is hosting us. It is an experience that aligns with the values of our community. With the values we aim to support about sharing, caring, growth and respect for nature. All participants will be asked to become a member of Gaia Terra (EUR 5 membership fee to pay upon arrival) and to contribute to the life of the Ecovillage with two 30 minutes slots of volunteering during their stay. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-smmkt-20260824000000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation Intensive With Rick Nodine: Training Ground, Autumn 26 Edition
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • London, Thursday October 29th to Sunday November 01st, 10h-17h In this intensive 4 days of study we will explore the ground that CI dances on. We will build our connect to earth, through the floor, with solo Somatics and simple but essential patterns that underpin the CI body. We will grow confidence in falling, rolling and inverting ourselves so that our partner dances can expand beyond habitual limits. Extended contact dancing guided by scores that demand connection through principles will guide us to the edges we need to work at. We will also reclaim the floor as playground for fresh explorations of the form and the pleasures of improvisation for its own sake. Time to explore, space to discover and days to build new skills will lead to questions and some answers about the dances you are looking for. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-shsgr-20261029100000

sessions.title: GROUNDWARDS CI Class with Emily Yuiming Wong & Jam Soundscape from David Leahy DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Goldsmiths University - room 154, Main Reception- Richard Hoggart Building, Lewisham Way, London, UK, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam finishing at 18:00. Groundwards We will explore ways of landing, in solos and together, softly, safely, dynamically. In journeying together towards the ground, it begins as a practice of surrendering (landing as resting, recharging, resetting, orienting), into possibilities. In the actions of rising and descending, we can play with timings, and release our expectations continuously and instantaneously, so each moment is the right moment as the ground unfolds our dance. Bio: Emily Yuiming enjoys dancing, improvising and movement/embodiment research. She has been involved with the CI scene in Beijing while she lived there for 6 years, teaching, learning, organising and performing. She currently teaches dance and is a practicing Craniosacral Therapist. She is interested in meditative and creative practices, with Aikido being the latest addition. Tickets available for the class & jam or separately at four different pricing options. Supporter - this rate supports the overheads and helps us invest in future events. If you are earning comfortably, support the arts. Regular - contributes to paying the teacher and musician. sessions.title: "FROM FORM TO FLOW" CI Class with Kate Brown & Jam Soundscape from Cy-An DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. From Form to Flow We will begin with simple bodywork in partners, reminding ourselves of the skeleton’s structure and facility and our own mass and weight. Then we will practice letting this weight fall into dancing through the easy folding and unfolding of the skeleton, using skills exercises to help, into the floor, into and with a partner, and within the whole ensemble. We will finish with a group improvisation, finding our own personal flow in and out of the dance. Kate first studied contact improvisation in 1981 at Middlesex Polytechnic. She has taught at London Contemporary Dance School, Laban, Independent Dance, Morley College. She and the late Rebecca Skelton curated LIP (London Improvisation in Performance) 2002 – 2008. Long-term collaborators include dancers Mira Hirtz, London; Lauren Potter, London, Hebden Bridge; Liverpool Improvisation Collective (Andrea Buckley, Paula Hampson, Mary Prestidge). Also writer Nicholas Ridout, most recently in # nine as part of the first Artists’ Archive, Siobhan Davies Studios. See the website for more details on our pricing tiers. sessions.details: Studio 5, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • Contact Improvisation Workshop – Beginners and Beyond Back to Basics: Simple Skills for Complex Situations In this foundational workshop, we will explore some of the foundational qualities of locomoting, falling, sensing, and relaxing, to allow play in solo and in contact dances. Expect to a repertoire of states to apply in class and/or jamming in the wild! Bring things you would like to work on, too. sessions.title: 'Flow, Fall, Fly' CI Class with Zoe Solomons & Jam Soundscape from Gloria Yehilevsky DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Flow, Fall, Fly In this class we will apply dynamic anatomical principles to help us find ease, flow and momentum in dancing. We will study patterns and tools that invite flow, to help us move between the ground and standing and make easy contact with partners. We will explore the gradient of flow vs solidity in service of comfortable weight sharing. This class references The Axis Syllabus, and Osteopathic understanding of the structure and function of the moving body for Contact Improvisation. ZOE may be creating a live improvised music score for class. Biography Zoë is a dancer/mother/teacher/Osteopath. Zoë has been been practicing and performing improvisation since 2000 and considers it a life practice as well as a professional skill. She has performed for Felix Ruckert, Tino Seghal and Simonetta Alessandri among others. Zoë qualified to teach Axis Syllabus universal motion principles in 2008, she combines this knowledge with anatomical understanding from her work as an osteopath in her teaching. She has taught at major dance centres and schools in the U.K. and internationally including, Earth Dance, Nomadic College, Emerging Hearts, The Place, Northern School of Contemporary Dance and many others. She is currently exploring Synaethesia in performance in her own work: The Perception Question See the website for more details on our pricing tiers. sessions.title: IN-SIGHT/IN-MOTION CI& FELDENKRAIS WORKSHOP with Simonetta Alessandri
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • In-Sight / In-Motion Contact Improvisation & Feldenkrais We will explore how our vision influences our movement and how this reflects in our Contact Improvisation practice. Through a Feldenkrais exploration, we will investigate how eye movement influences whole-body coordination, orientation, and balance. Since vision and kinaesthetic information work together, sharpening how the brain processes visual information improves overall coordination, movement, and self-awareness. We will then explore the spectrum of visual possibilities in movement, ranging from closed eyes and soft peripheral vision to sustained eye contact and focused direct gazing. We will observe how these experiences alter how we navigate the confluence of inputs—from both the kinesthetic experience of physical contact and the various forms of visual information. We will draw upon this process to refine and deepen our practice, allowing intuitive responses to emerge spontaneously while also practicing specific technical solutions. Bio Simonetta is an Italian dance artist and a somatic educator based in London. She is an internationally recognised teacher that applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance and movement training as well as performance making. Her work is informed by more than 35 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Since 1991 Simonetta has been practicing, performing and teaching Contact improvisation. She kept Contact Improvisation alive in Rome for more than ten years through her classes and Jams. Currently she teaches at London Contemporary Dance School, Trinity Laban, and Goldsmiths University. She olds the Post Graduated Diploma from London Contemporary Dance School, she is qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method. She is a Fellow of the Higher Education Academy. She is one of the founders of CI@Goldsmiths, association of dance artists dedicated to the promotion and dissemination of Contact Improvisation in London and UK. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sh1rl-20260131110000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Touchdown Dance
sessions.details: Goldsmiths University - room 154, Main Reception- Richard Hoggart Building, Lewisham Way, London, UK, SE14 6NW • Touchdown Dance ONE DAY workshop. Touchdown Dance workshops are uniquely devised to bring visually Impaired and sighted people together in movement and dance. Touchdown Dance workshops are an experience! Based in CI fundamentals devised by Steve Paxton and Anne Kilcoyne at Dartington in 1986 – 1994 and developed by the company in workshops, partnership projects and festivals over the decades. CI is based in the fusion of yoga, dance and Aikido and gymnastics. As a movement discipline, you discover a greater awareness of your body, posture and range of movement potential. Contact Improvisation involves working in physical contact with others to sense and feel our own weight, energy, and our ability to move together safely. Touchdown dance specialist workshop leaders are visually impaired and sighted, teaching through demonstration, audio description and consensual hands-on facilitation methods. We teach warm up and meet and greet exercises, that are fun and work with partnering and group explorations. The aim is to deepen your trust in your ability to move through space, to gain improved orientation skills and confidence in joining participatory physical activity. We work with principles and specialist inclusive methods developed over many years of research since 1986 – see our book Inclusive Dance, the story of Touchdown Dance, published by Intellectbooks.com ACCESS -Touchdown Dance is experienced in providing accessible sessions, we have worked in collaboration with Action for Blind, Henshaws Society for the Blind,SENSE, and many other organisations, nationally and internationally. -Guide Dogs are welcome and there is a walking area around the building. FOR access support please contact Katy Dymoke and Holly Thomas at info@touchdowndance.co.uk or call Katy 07932038730, For more about us go to our website; www.touchdowndance.co.uk https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sg5hz-20260208110000

sessions.title: Earthflow" CI Class with Akeim Toussaint Buck with & Jam Soundscape from TBC DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Earthflow I call my classes Earthflow, as my intention in my physical practice is to feel connected to the ground whilst seeking to find flow and expansion in my movement. Expect some set material and improvisation stimuli to investigate and interrogate. This is an approach inspired by Contemporary and Caribbean Dance Elements, Release Technique, Animal Flow, Yoga, Elements of Hip Hop and Capoeira. Together we seek liberation and flow, strengthening our connection to the earth both physically and energetically, seeking to find elasticity in our movement. We'll creatively explore cyclical movement patterns and rhythms, travelling through the space, inviting curiosity, accuracy, breath and clarity. In an energetic and playful environment, we'll revitalise our senses, build strength, stamina and open our awareness as we move together as a community. Akeim Toussaint Buck is an award-winning interdisciplinary performer and maker, born in Jamaica and raised in England. Founder of Toussaint To Move, his work blends dance, voice, and multiple art forms to explore socio-political realities through visceral, participatory performance grounded in improvisation and holistic movement practice. sessions.title: 'BRAIDING BASS, BODY & SPACE' CI class with Nathaniel Parchment & Jam Soundscape from CYAN
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • 'BRAIDING BASS, BODY & SPACE' This CLASS offers a generative warm-up and preparation for the contact improvisation jam, creating space for embodied exploration that honours non-hierarchical ways of knowing and being. Drawing on indigenous onto-epistemologies that recognize the intelligence and agency of all bodies—human and more-than-human—we'll engage with movement practices that dissolve boundaries between self and other, internal and external, doing and being. Through somatic investigations and light movement scores we'll attune to the subtle resonances that connect us: the rhythms of our own bodies - breath and heartbeat, the pulse of the collective, the vibration of sound and the sentient space we share. This approach invites us into a liberatory mode of attention where sensation, impulse, and communication flow freely with curiosity and play. Bio Nathaniel is a London-based dance artist whose interests lie at the intersections of embodied enquiry, African diasporic and queer social dance cultures, global body-mind practices and performance. In 2023 he graduated from the MA in Creative Practice: Dance Professional programme, delivered by Trinity Laban in partnership with Independent Dance (ID) and Siobhan Davies Studios, joining ID as Co-Director in the same year. Nathaniel is currently working as a freelance teacher, producer and choreographer, developing research into decolonising dance pedagogies and reparative performance research. He is part of a dance and music collective at K5 Studios, Harringay Warehouse District and runs the regular Global Majority Underscore workshop with Siobhan Davies Studios Co-Artistic Director Annie Lok at Pembroke House, South London. sessions.title: 'Dance Improvisation Practices that offer Resilience and Support in Difficult Times' Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Kirstie Simson
sessions.details: Laban Building - Car Park Studio, Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ • Dance Improvisation Practices that offer Resilience and Support in Difficult Times There is deep inspiration and rigour in a practice of improvisation that posits vulnerability at its heart. Developing the skills to be able to care for, engage, respect, and respond to that state of openness in oneself, others and our environment generates creative work that is powerful and transformative. Learning from a place of interconnectedness can give us the resilience and insight to face life's challenges and complexities. In this workshop Kirstie shares the movement practices and underlying philosophies she has developed over forty years of her committed involvement with dance improvisation as a ‘life- practice’ that has helped her negotiate life’s ups and downs. Integral with the physical practices she will elucidate how she views embodied practices as vitally important for developing the resilience to face into and respond to the challenging times we are living through. Having gone through recent life-threatening illness herself, Kirstie draws firsthand on how grounded dance practices can guide and sustain us as we face into life’s greatest challenges, and the life-affirming health benefits they offer us. Kirstie draws from her extensive experience of Contact Improvisation, dance techniques, the Alexander technique, Aikido, meditation, and her broad knowledge of improvisation in performance. She shares her work through facilitated exercises, open time for play and exploration, movement scores, observations, self-reflection, deep listening, discussion, and humour. Much of the work is experienced through partnering and connection with others, balanced with solo time for processing and reflection. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sc968-20260308110000

sessions.title: BETWEEN SELF & OTHER CI Class with Laura Doehler & Jam Soundscape from TBC DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Between self and other In the shift from control to responsiveness, dancing begins; not by holding on, but by sensing what is and how your intention towards a certain kind of connection becomes a shared experience. We will inquire how awareness and intentional listening allow us to navigate touch and shared weight for an inclusive and consensual practice where partners, space, and our own desires and capacities to move are incorporated. Laura Doehler began her Contact Improvisation practice in 2004 with Kirstie Simson and soon connected to the wider international CI community, learning with pioneers such as Steve Paxton, Nancy Stark-Smith, Karen Nelson and many others who expanded the form into poetic, political, and somatic inquiries. She is a London-based dance artist having studied dance and performance making at Trinity Laban (2004) and Goldsmiths University (2012) and she is a recipient of the Trinity Laban scholarship. Her work as dance artist and director of Exit Map is firmly rooted in CI and improvisation. Her work is collaborative and participatory in nature and explores collective agency, scores of co-existence and care and proposes new socio-environmental relational practices such as outdoor dance events, rituals and embodied co-working spaces. In 2012 she co-founded Shared Practice with Anne-Gaelle Thiriot and Tania Soubry, which is an ongoing improvisation research project that she facilitates besides teaching in Higher Education at NCCA and independent workshops across Europe. Over the past years she connected to community organisations that work with CI and involve rural land practices. Recent residencies include Radical Inclusion and Architectures of Togetherness with Ponderosa e.V. in Germany, Catalysing Collective Action at The Place in London 2024 and soon with Larret En Mouvement as part of Larretude a research around Agentic Assemblages (FR). sessions.title: 'ATTUNEMENT & SUPPORT' Beginners and Beyond CI-Workshop with Thomas Kampe at Chisenhale Dance Space
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • ATTUNEMENT & SUPPORT Please note. This workshop will be held at Chisenhale Dance Space Between 3pm and 7pm Chisenhale Dance Space https://share.google/PqMkGL9UFlA2OO1jm Beginners and Beyond CI Workshop with Thomas Kampe This workshop introduces Contact Improvisation through principles of the Feldenkrais Method®. Through gentle movement explorations and guided partner work, we develop sensitivity to touch, weight, and timing, discovering greater ease and spontaneity in our dancing. Feldenkrais-based Awareness Through Movement® supports clearer relationship with gravity in partnership situations including weight bearing, carrying and lifting situations. As we refine how we give, share and receive weight, we cultivate embodied listening, co-regulation, and creative responsiveness in more skills-oriented CI dancing. Open to beginners and experienced movers alike, this workshop invites curiosity, connection, and joyful shared movement. Thomas Kampe (PhD; UK/GER) lives in London and has worked with dance, theatre, performance and movement for the last 45 years. He has worked as performer, choreographer and director in Germany and Britain, and was Professor for Somatic Performance and Education at Bath Spa University, UK until 2022. Since the late 1980’s Thomas has taught somatic approaches towards Movement Education, Theatre and Contact Improvisation in different settings around the world. Thomas is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method® which forms a foundation for his teaching of dance and movement. His numerous research writings and performance works have been published internationally. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-s4dop-20260321150000

sessions.title: 'Dancing from Zero' CI Class with Danny Kearns & Jam Soundscape
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Dancing from zero Focusing on coming back to a sensation of our bodies, where we can revel in the small details available to our perceptions. Allowing us to meet other partners in the dance with sensitivity and curiosity. The effort is to recognise our patterns and to slow down, is it possible to receive before responding? Once the connection is established we can challenge our awareness with varying size and speed of motion. Daniel Kearns writes, dances and creates theatre with close collaborators. He is interested in the states we can achieve through performance and ritual, finding pathways to deeper connections and transcending our ordinary borders. He graduated in Physical Theatre from East 15 Acting school in London and worked for 4 years in Poland with companies in the lineage of director Jerzy Grotowski. He currently works with Petya Stoykova and Dune Dance Company performing in ‘Sky Earth’ and their new Project ‘Field’. Since 2008 he has practiced Brazilian Art-form Capoeira and Contact-Improvisation, both of which continue to inspire and influence his teaching in movement and theatre. sessions.title: "SENSATIONAL MOVES": 4 DAY INTENSIVE Feldenkrais, Movement Exploration & CI with Simonetta Alessandri & Thomas Kampe
sessions.details: Laban Building - Car Park Studio, Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ • 9 -12 April 4 day Intensive exploring somatic dance practice, Feldenkrais Method and Contact Improvisation. In this four-day workshop, we combine somatic movement principles of the Feldenkrais Method® with dance exploration & Contact Improvisation practice. In the morning sessions we will be using ‘Awareness Through Movement’® lessons and touch-based explorations. We will focus on solo explorations and will connect with our core and imagination, deepening our felt sense of gravity and grounding. Through Dance Improvisational practice we will develop our observation skills and individual creative agency to integrate our embodied discoveries, fostering creative risk-taking and playfulness. In the afternoon sessions we will expand these explorations into the practice of Contact Improvisation to enhance our ability to interact and communicate expressively and playfully with ease and clarity. Drawing on our felt sense and somatic attunement through touch and gravity, we will revisit and refine Contact Improvisation skills and creative vocabularies, such as weight exchange, balance, support and lifts with a partner and in small ensemble situations. Each day we will take time to discuss or write reflectively about our personal and somatic discoveries. The Feldenkrais Method®, developed by Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais (1904-1984) is a gentle, embodied process focused on self-discovery, embodied thinking, creative wellbeing, and functional improvement. It invites participants to slow down, listen to internal sensations, and create new neural pathways that support creative choice making in action. This workshop is open to anyone interested in deepening their dance or movement practice. Thomas and Simonetta have been teaching Feldenkrais-informed dance classes around the globe for over 20 years and are world-leading researchers and artists in the field. Dr.Thomas Kampe (PhD) (he/him) has worked as a performer, artist, researcher and somatic educator across the globe for 40 years and was ‘Professor of Somatic Performance and Education’ at Bath Spa University (UK) between 2012 and 2022. Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260409100000

sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv Jam: non-duet jam @Centre 151!
sessions.details: Centre 151, 151 Whiston Road, London, E2 8GU • Note there is a change of time and venue: 6.30-8.30pm at Centre 151. Close to Haggerston station. This weekly jam invites movers to jam together for a regular practice. For this special jam the participants are invited to dance either in solo or with more than one partner. Going beyond the usual duet form we will co-create inter-dependent group constellations moving through space. Warming-up: Marie Chabert will facilitate the warming-up before the jam. The aim is for participants to arrive into their body, tune in, connect to themselves, to others and to the space around. It brings people into their senses and embodied awareness. It is not a class and doesn't give the same technical tools and principles as a class. Classes are held every other week and will give you these skills. Jam The emphasis of the jam is to explore movement while being in physical contact with other movers. Participants are encouraged to experience it as an embodied research and a practice where everyone can bring their own research, interest and physicality into the space. Jamming is the best way to try out, take risks, learn from each other, observe, connect, be surprised, practice setting boundaries, dance and play with people we may not know or expect to dance with. Participants are asked to take responsibility for themselves. Levels and abilities: The space welcomes professional dancers, amateurs, seasoned contact improvisers and beginners who took a few classes beforehand, people from all backgrounds and abilities. It requires every participants to take responsibility for their own bodies, so if you need extra support or a carer or someone to look after you then you can bring them for free. Note for beginners: the jam is not guided. The facilitator is there to hold the space but doesn't guide the jam. What is Contact Improvisation (CI)? Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. This practice emphasises spontaneity, trust, and mutual awareness, allowing dancers to create unique movement for each encounter. Connect to the community If you want to join the CI Monday Jam WhatsApp group, click on this link: https://chat.whatsapp.com/Iivbz8LzDvK5lGkiitQgcH (when you join please introduce yourself so we know who's in the chat) More events on mariechabert.com https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sewmd-20251222183000

sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv : Contemplative Practice into Silent Jam
sessions.details: Rose Lipman Building, 43 De Beauvoir Road, London, N1 • This weekly jam invites movers to jam together for a regular practice. For this one, no late commers will be admitted after 6.40pm! The Contemplative Dance Practice was developed by the American dancer, choreographer and Buddhist Barbara Dilley in the 70's, 80's. There will be a clear structure: -Opening circle and explanation of the structure -Sitting Meditation (15 min), -Personal Awareness Practice (15 min) -Open Space into Silent Jam (until 8.15pm) -Close-up circle (finishes at 8.30pm) Jam The emphasis of the jam is to explore movement while being in physical contact with other movers. sessions.title: **Contact Improv & Philosophy Playshop: DANCING... GENDER-LESS!**
sessions.details: Chisenhale Dance Space, 64-84 Chisenhale Road, London, E3 5QZ • A playshop on undoing gender in and through contact improvisation This Playshop (workshop/playful lab) is led by Marie Chabert and Dr Noah Martin (Women's Touch meets the College of modern anxiety) How does gender come into your dance? Many of us experience tremendous liberation from the constraints of gender roles through contact improv; this playshop from Marie Chabert and Dr. Noah Martin will explore ways to bring that kind of freedom into the rest of your life and your relationships, as well as how we can open ourselves up to even greater self-expression and even deeper ways of relating to each other in the dance space. This playshop will involve: Movement games to help reveal ways that gender shapes patterns of movement and ways of relating Philosophical ideas to help us understand the role of gender in our lives Time for self-reflection and journaling …and of course, time to jam! https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sk4q-20260314140000

sessions.title: **Women’s Touch : Community as a relational process with Marie Chabert & Manuela Albrecht**
sessions.details: Siobhan Davies Studios, 85 Saint George's Road, London, SE1 6ER • Embodiment - Safe space - Community Develop self-enquiry and responsibility- Be in touch - Share impressions + experiences + food For this edition Marie Chabert is inviting the dance practitioner Manuela Albrecht to share her beautiful research on mycelium and inter-connectivity. Manuela and Marie will facilitate this Contact Impro workshop/laboratory exploring how community forms through relation and difference. Weaving between movement scores and moments of shared reflection, participants are invited to experience connection in and out of contact using yarns, a soft fibrous material used for crochet. We will open up questions and investigations such as: -How do I enter and inhabit a group? -What helps me feel connected without erasing difference? -Is community something we belong to, or something we continuously make? Work unfolds through solo and group scores, supporting attentive connection, experimentation, and self-enquiry. The space intends to be safe, consent-based, and accessible to all. No prior experience with Contact Improvisation is required. Rough schedule (may change slightly) 11.30am-12pm – Welcome tea & chat (meeting each other + time & space to land) 12pm –1.30pm – Intro to Consent, solo warm-up and collective embodied explorations 1.30-2.30pm – Lunchtime: food sharing 2.30-5.30pm – Group score playing with the yarns. Small group reflection: community as a relational process Witnessing, writing, sharing and integrating Who is this for? Those who identify as women and non-binary interested in bringing more awareness to physical interactions in dancing and in Contact Improvisation It welcomes all levels and can be applied to other practices than Contact Improvisation. In the past people were able to apply it to dance therapy, theatre, psychology... What is Women's Touch? Women’s touch is an event curated by Marie Chabert, that offers a space for those who identify as women and non-binary to explore movement practices that are mainly based on touch (Contact Improvisation, somatic practices, improvisation) in a safe environment. It aims to empower women to claim their space and redefine it within a framework that challenges prevailing patriarchal norms. It offers both a place to embody and share our experiences verbally. The intention is to foster a safe and supportive environment, where women and non-binary participants feel safe to take risks, be vulnerable, and share their humanity, free from the pressure of dominant dynamics. Cultivating such spaces requires trust, shared language, and mutual care. It allows us not just to survive, but to thrive in connection. Manuela's bio: I’m a Brazilian interdisciplinary movement artist, facilitator, performer, and PhD researcher working at the intersection of dance, theatre, community, and micro-politics. My practice explores movement as a way to reflect on the urgencies of our time. I’m especially drawn to collaborative processes and to disrupt colonial legacies, the normative, and stay radically tender, embodied, and real. https://manualbrecht5.wixsite.com/manualbrecht/ If you have financial difficulties and want to take part, please get in touch. photo by Sol Ji Park https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sv4ry-20260510233000

Event page: https://millco.co.uk/events/contact-improvisation-jam/ https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-scnfy-20260615183000

sessions.title: The Dancing Body - Fundamentals in CI
sessions.details: Solonda Studio, 53 High Street, Ilfracombe, EX34 9QB • A day workshop offering an introduction into some of the fundamental principles and techniques of Contact Improvisation as a dance practice. We’ll be gently warming up our dancing bodies in solo exercises before exploring what it means to go in and out of Contact and into a dance with others. Contact Improvisation as a form of dance/sport offers us an opportunity for embodied expression and relation, and of finding a flow with oneself and others. As it is a very open practice it can be suitable to anyone, including those with no prior experience. You can expect gentle guided exercises and experiences that aim to demystify the practice and to open us to improvising together, and will include movement, touch and time for reflection. Please do contact us in advance with any access needs. We’ll be happy to hear from you and want to make this workshop open and accessible. Sole de la Hoz teaches Contact Improvisation in London and festivals in the Uk (eg. Temenos Dance, The Sanctuary, CI@Goldsmiths, London Contact Festival, Acland Burghley). She trained at Rambert School of Ballet and Contemporary Dance, and works as a professional dancer in opera (ROH, ENO, Glyndebourne) as well as immersive productions and screen. She has a life dedicated to movement and continues her learning journey through regularly training in London and abroad, she is deeply grateful to her teachers. Alongside performing and Contact Improvisation Sole is a trainee psychotherapist in Process Oriented Psychology, Yoga Instructor, Thai Masseuse Mark Rietema teaches Somatics and CI in London and abroad, and offers workshops, lectures and group facilitation around embodiment, dance and mental health in places such as King’s College, the NHS and grassroots groups. He works as a psychotherapist and somatic movement educator, having studied Process Oriented Psychology and Body-Mind Centering®. His previous background is in community arts projects and performances (UK, US) with an MA in Community Arts from Goldsmiths. He teaches as faculty for Embody Move UK, Institut Prozessarbeit Deutschland, as an associate in King's College, and as a guest teacher in various institutions. He recently completed an Erasmus research project around creative pedagogies in higher education. www.markrietema.com https://bookwhen.com/markrietema/e/ev-s6xk3-20260523100000

sessions.title: London CI Weekender - 2-days of Contact Improvisation, Exploring the concepts of 'reaching and meeting' and 'elements of the jam' w/ Josh Jones and Lily Antonia
sessions.details:
Willesden, London, UK •

London CI Weekender
A 2-day Contact Improvisation Workshop

https://dandelion.events/e/londonciweekender

Whats on offer :

A weekend of movement, exploration, co-collaboration and connection. Diving into the fundamentals & philosophy of Contact Improvisation. The London CI weekender offers space and guidance to engage with the practice, tune into the body & develop the skills that serve us both in the dance and in daily life. Bringing shared inquiry to the foreground to drop deeper through the layers of what is possible through CI.

Each day has a distinct focus, and participants are welcome to attend either day or the full weekend.

Come deepen your connection to the ground, space, and others while gaining tools for (contact) improvisation, jamming, and attuned awareness through the joy of movement.

Saturday: Reaching & Meeting

How can we meet the earth, meet ourselves and meet another?

How is it to be met?

Investigating and understanding mutual meeting as a core part of the dance.

Reaching as a tool to transform into oscillating expansive movers. To expand awareness and expand the ability of the body. Coming away from collapse and into lightness. Effortlessly working with gravity and momentum.

The day will include technical insights and the approach is practice as research. Allowing ourselves to be informed by the dance - discover how to move functionally and fluidly, open to all that's available in the present. Through solo, partner & group exercises we’ll explore reaching and meeting as a shared point of attention.

This day offers a grounding in presence, deep listening, and mutual direction.

Sunday: Elements of the Jam


On Sunday, we move into the world of the jam. We’ll explore how to enter and exit dances, navigate shared space, and respond to each unfolding moment. Through structured improvisation scores and instant compositions, we’ll build tools for the jam, develop adaptability, and co-create through group improvisation.

A container for emergence and spontaneity.

Meet your facilitators

Lily Antonia (She/Her)

Dance & Movement Artist | Facilitator

Lily is a contemporary dance artist, specialising in contact improvisation. She has been performing, teaching and choreographing internationally since graduating from Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance. Working with companies Wired Aerial Theatre, Mad Dogs Dance Theatre, and Company Chameleon. Lily is co-director of contemporary dance and live art company UMAMI-i!-MOVEMENT and co-founder of Contact Improv MCR.

Her approach to CI blends playful exploration with technical clarity. Inviting curiosity, challenging potential and nurturing individual creativity - Lily invites dancers to bring an aliveness to the present. Creating spaces where dancers can listen, trust, and move with intention and spontaneity.

“To cultivate a state of being - in readiness without anticipation... we open ourselves to

all possibilities"

Joshua Jarvis Jones (He/They)

Dancer | Facilitator | Choreographer

Josh is a contemporary dancer and facilitator whose practice is rooted in emotional exploration and embodied connection. Following a time of personal loss, he found dance—especially contact improvisation—as a vital tool for healing, grounding, and rediscovering relationships with self and others.

Trained at the Northern School of Contemporary Dance, Josh has performed internationally with artists like Ricardo Buscarini and Madeline Shan, exploring themes of intimacy and identity. His deep engagement with contact improvisation bridges technique and relational psychology, and he shares this through workshops, labs, and festivals worldwide.

Josh co founded *Lean_In*, a nomadic movement series that has grown into the annual retreat *Lean_In_Nature*, inviting people into co-creative, embodied research. He has taught at CI hubs and circus events across Europe. His work invites participants into a dance of authenticity, where movement becomes a meeting place for truth and transformation.

INCLUSIVE AND SAFE SPACE

We are committed to creating an inclusive and safe space for all participants. This workshop is designed to be accessible to all and participants are welcome to let us know in advance about specific access needs.

Who’s the workshop for

Open to all levels. Whether you’re a seasoned pro or you've never done the practice before there is something for everyone. No previous dance experience required just a willingness to explore & create.

Where

Location will be given upon booking.

We will be dancing in the heart of Camberwell, the space has a kitchen available for use by participants, toilets and showers. Access to the hall is via a flight of stairs.

If you would like to attend but have financial concerns / are unable to purchase a ticket due to financial reasons please reach out - we will find the way with you.

This event is 18+

If you have any queries / questions / thoughts or just want to say hi please feel free to get in touch - leaninmovement123@gmail.com

https://dandelion.events/e/londonciweekender

In this gentle workshop, we’ll offer simple, playful ways to connect through movement. Contact Improvisation, also called CI, can include touch, sharing weight, or dynamic motion, but we will keep everything light, optional, and based on comfort.

sessions.title: Weaving Spatial Support into the Body, Laura Doehler
sessions.details: Rockingham Community Centre, Falmouth Rd, London SE1 6PD, UK • In this workshop we will explore how our weight always is supported by default and acts as support simply through being in space and sensing our own physical space. We strip back expectations around support to uncover how a changing physical landscape of body and space create a container that at times carries us through space and at other times means we become a carrier or both. Our intention is to challenge our ideas around support so we won't get locked into carrying or loading and acquire greater skills in falling through letting go and expansion. We will probe spatial anchoring by tapping into the expansive qualities that space offers, find lightness and the ability to redirect oneself. These processes shall support a greater freedom in our dancing inside and outside the studio as we reckon with our ideas and expectations around support.

sessions.title: Cuban Roots & Social Dance Secrets with Osbanis & Anneta 🇨🇺 🎶
sessions.details: O2 Centre,The Venue Hall, O2 Centre,The Venue Hall, 255 Finchley Road, London, NW3 6LU, United Kingdom • ✨ NEW 6 WEEKS COURSE, CUBAN ROOTS & SOCIAL DANCE SECRETS ✨ Elevate your social dancing! This 6-week course is designed to deepen your understanding of Cuban dance by exploring its rich roots while unlocking the secrets that make social dancing feel natural, confident, and enjoyable. We will focus on authentic Cuban movement, musicality, and cultural context, helping you understand why we dance the way we do, not just the steps themselves. Alongside this, you’ll learn practical social dance skills that improve connection, flow, and communication on the dance floor. Expect a balance of technique, rhythm, and partner work, with clear guidance on how to apply everything in real social dance settings. This course is ideal for dancers who want to refine their foundations, gain confidence, and dance with more ease and expression. What you’ll gain: 👉 A deeper connection to Cuban dance roots and musical structure 👉 Improved body movement, rhythm, and coordination 👉 Practical tools for smoother, more confident social dancing 👉 Greater awareness, connection, and enjoyment on the dance floor 📍 Open to all levels with basic foundations Join us and dance with confidence, rhythm, and sabor! PRICE: FULL COURSE- £120 (6 weeks, 12h) *£25 per 1 week session The deposit of £30 needs to be paid by 26 January in order to secure your place on the course. The spaces are limited so please contact us ASAP to confirm your participation.

sessions.title: Casino Libre
sessions.details: Farr’s Dalston, 17-19 Dalston Ln,, London, E8 3DF, United Kingdom • Casino (Cuban Salsa) Partnerwork Classes Mondays | 7:00–11:00 PM Drop-in classes | 3 Levels Join us for a focused evening of Casino (Cuban Salsa) partnerwork, dedicated to building strong foundations, clean technique, and confident social dancing all rooted in authentic Cuban style. These drop-in classes are designed to support dancers at different stages, with three progressive levels running back-to-back so you can join exactly where you feel comfortable. Class Structure Intro: For beginners and those new to Casino. Learn the essential basics of Cuban partnerwork, connection, flow, and movement in a friendly, supportive environment. Improvers: For dancers with some experience. Strengthen your foundations, refine technique, and develop better flow, timing, and partner communication. Basics. For confident dancers ready to deepen their skills. Explore more complex partnerwork, musical interpretation, variation, and technique with a strong focus on clarity, control, and social usability. What to Expect Clear, structured teaching Strong emphasis on correct Casino technique Partner rotation (no need to bring a partner change partners) A relaxed but focused atmosphere Practical material you can use immediately on the social dance floor Our approach prioritises quality over quantity, helping you build a solid technical base while staying true to the social, playful spirit of Cuban dance. Whether you’re just starting out or looking to sharpen your skills, these classes offer a consistent space to learn, practice, and grow.

sessions.title: Rueda Libre Wednesday
sessions.details: Coal Drops Yard, Coal Drops Yard, King's Cross, London, N1C 4DQ, United Kingdom • Join Us for an Unforgettable Outdoor Cuban Salsa Event! Get ready to dance, learn, meet amazing people, and embrace the joy of salsa dancing! Our outdoor salsa event is the perfect place to spice up your evening, whether you’re a seasoned pro or have never danced a step in your life. Here’s what to expect: Cuban Salsa and Rueda Classes: We’re offering classes for all levels, from absolute beginners to advanced dancers. No partner? No problem! Everyone is welcome to join in the fun. Visit https://ruedalibre.co.uk/classes/ to find out which class level suits you best. Class Time: Classes kick off at 7:00 PM, but make sure to arrive by 6:45 PM to show your QR code and collect your wristband. This ensures you’re ready and wont miss the beginning of the class. Free Outdoor Social Dancing: After the class, the night doesn’t end! Stick around for some free outdoor social dancing. Practice your moves, connect with the community, and just let loose. The vibe is 10/10 so enjoy it. Affordable Pricing: Classes start at just £9 (excluding fees), and you’ll have the chance to practice what you’ve learned during the session. The most budget-friendly way to pay is via our QR code in person, no fees to worry about. Prefer other options? We also accept cash, contactless payments, and online payments for your convenience. Whether you’re a salsa enthusiast or simply looking for a new experience, this is your chance to dance, laugh, and create unforgettable memories. Join us and make the most of your Wednesday evening. Ready to dance? Grab your spot and let’s make this a night to remember. See you there!

sessions.details: Jazz Cafe, 5 Parkway Camden Town, London, NW1 7PG, United Kingdom • London’s Sambroso All Stars light the fuse on a night of pure Cuban fire — a full live band steeped in rhythm, colour and joy. Driving percussion, punchy brass and lock-tight grooves collide, turning the room into something closer to a Havana dancefloor than a gig. It’s music that moves first and asks questions later. Drawing from salsa, rumba and Latin jazz, this all-star collective channels the spirit of Cuba with swagger, precision and serious soul — played by musicians who live and breathe it.

sessions.title: Fresto Live at The Dark Horse Stables
sessions.details: The Dark Horse Camden, 87/88 North Yard, London, England, NW1 8AH, United Kingdom • For the first time ever, Cuban artist Fresto brings his unmistakable voice, raw emotion, and Caribbean soul to London city, marking a historic debut that promises to be nothing short of unforgettable. Known for turning heartbreak into anthems and passion into melody, Fresto arrives with a sound that has already crossed oceans — now ready to echo through one of the world’s most iconic music capitals. From the viral power of “Me Hace Daño Verte” to the romantic pulse of his latest work, Fresto’s music blends Cuban roots, modern salsa, and heartfelt storytelling into a live experience that goes far beyond a concert — it’s a connection. This London debut is not just another date on a tour; it’s a moment for fans who have waited to feel his songs come alive on stage. Expect intensity. Expect intimacy. Expect the kind of performance that reminds you why live music still matters

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Change: Julia Testas
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Julia's improvisation sessions are inspired by a combination of her own somatic research, 'dissolving', and David Zambrano's concept of 'passing through'. Connecting, sharing and collaborating, Julia invites you to tap into your body's intelligence and enhance your self-awareness towards cultivating greater confidence and freedom in your movement. This week we'll explore the sacrum, the pelvis and psoas to find inner freedom and dance it all!

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Manuela Albrecht
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Manuela is a Brazilian movement artist, facilitator, performer, researcher, and mother. Drawing from physical theatre, contemporary dance, somatic practices and her lived experience of samba and the Brazilian carnival, she channels movement into nurturing creativity, curiosity, inventiveness and self-expression. She is particularly interested in exploring solo and group improvisational dynamics as a door to cultivate listening, acceptance, playfulness and care.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Rosie Noctor
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Rosie is a dancer, a creative and a mother of two. She welcomes you into her class with grounding practices, mindful awareness and focused breathing. The class integrates somatic practices, formal contemporary dance techniques and inspiration from the Ilan Lev Method. "During my class I hope to create a metaphorical cocoon in which you can have a direct experience of your body and mind in its current state, however you come".

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Sunanda Biswas AKA Bgirl SUNSUN
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Sunanda is one of the UK's most accomplished Jazz and Hip Hop dance artists. In this session, she'll bring us Lindy Hop, introducing the social and cultural history of the dance in its form and style of traditional and vernacular jazz. We'll explore the solo side with stomping and posture drills, then learn about lead and follow roles, developing into the 'swing out' and a short partner routine.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Rose Aida Sall Sao
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Rose is a Dutch-Senegalese dance artist based in London whose work explores the body as a site of memory, liberation and poetic expression. She will guide an improvisation in which we'll explore the space between stimulus and response, moving from a place of listening and presence. Our eyes and spine will be the driving foreces, grounding us as we shift between textures and intensities, discovering movement that surprises us. The class encourages authentic, playful and expressive movement.

sessions.details: Instructor: Inga George | Level: Intermediate | Room: Studio 2 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com.CLASS DESCRIPTION A moving class created for students with previous ballet experience and familiarity with ballet terminology. Progress through barre and center work designed to increase strength, flexibility, and technique and dance through graceful combinations. Special attention to epaulement (turning of the shoulders and upper body), correct training of the arms (port de bras), fluidity, placement, coordination with the head and all movements and precise coordination of the whole body, working as one unit.Recommended for those with around one year’s worth of knowledge of basic ballet steps or for returning students looking to go back to the basics. Minimum age: 16 (12+ as long as they have prior experience)

CLASS DESCRIPTION This class delivers an approach to partnering where participants from all experience levels are on an equal playing field. Always wanted to try ballet but didn’t know where to start? Or maybe you danced as a child and want to return to the basics? This class is designed just for you.Absolute Beginners Ballet is a slow-paced, supportive introduction to classical ballet. You’ll learn the essential positions, steps, and techniques, all while developing strength, flexibility, posture, and coordination. No experience is needed—just a willingness to learn and move. Each session includes foundational barre work and simple centre exercises to help you feel confident and graceful, even as a beginner. We keep things light and enjoyable, blending technique with fun, flowing movement. Whether you’re curious about ballet or simply looking for a new way to move your body, this class offers a welcoming space to begin. You’ll improve your posture, balance, coordination and overall strength and flexibility.”

You can expect a European-style ballet class influenced by Vaganova épaulement with just a touch of Balanchine speed and clarity. With an emphasis on “dancing,” Patricia likes to challenge the dancers with choreographic combinations that work not only the body, but the brain as well, and help them to use breath, dynamics, and musicality to make their dancing stand out through her corrections. Anyone looking to improve is welcome to Patricia’s class. Whether you are a contemporary or classically-trained dancer, a student or adult — just be ready to work hard, get sweaty and have fun!” Minimum age: 13

sessions.title: Ballet - Classical Ballet - Kim Amundsen - GEN - £12
sessions.details: Instructor: Kim Amundsen | Level: General | Room: Studio 2 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. Concessions and packages are available.Please note if you have to cancel your booking for any reason, our cancellation policy is 24 hours for open classes , 48 hours for masterclasses . For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com. CLASS DESCRIPTIONElevate your technique in an eclectic class that spans the globe. Drawing from his international career, Kim blends the structural precision of Vaganova with the signature speed and clarity of the Balanchine style.This isn't just a technical drill it’s a dance experience. Kim focuses on choreographic combinations that challenge your mental agility and physical expression. Through personalized corrections centered on breath and musicality, you’ll learn to move with a professional edge. Whether you’re a classical purist, a musical theatre performer, or a contemporary dancer, come prepared to work hard, sweat, and find the joy in the movement.This Class is Not Suitable for Absolute BeginnersBIOGRAPHYKim Amundsen is a highly accomplished and qualified Higher Education dance instructor with 12 years of experience planning and delivering dance curricula at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Born in Bodø, Norway, Kim trained at the Oslo National Academy of Dance and the Heinz-Bosl-Stiftung Ballet Academy University of Music and Performing Arts Munich. He has performed with world-renowned companies, including Bavarian State Ballet, Wayne Sleep’s World of Classical Ballet, Random Dance and New adventures.Amundsen is an expert in Classical Ballet (Vaganova, Ceccetti, RAD) Contemporary Dance (Limón) and Boris Kniaseff Floor Barre method. He has a proven ability to foster critical thinking, creative exploration, and advanced technical skills in diverse student populations.Amundsen is a Lecturer of Dance & Personal Tutor at Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance. He develops and delivers comprehensive curricula for undergraduate students, encompassing Classical Ballet, Contemporary Dance, and Kniaseff Floor Barre method, across Foundation to Postgraduate levels. He also leads and mentors’ students in artistic and technical development, fostering a disciplined and explorative learning environment. Kim also teaches at Acosta Advanced Training Huh. Teaching The next generation of talent Kniaseff Floor Barre method and Classical Ballet technique. Kim is a highly sought-after dance instructor and mentor.

Progress through barre and center work designed to increase strength, flexibility, and technique and dance through graceful combinations. Special attention to epaulement (turning of the shoulders and upper body), correct training of the arms (port de bras), fluidity, placement, coordination with the head and all movements and precise coordination of the whole body, working as one unit.Recommended for those with around one year’s worth of knowledge of basic ballet steps or for returning students looking to go back to the basics. Minimum age: 16 (12+ as long as they have prior experience)

CLASS DESCRIPTION Originating from the Dominican Republic, Bachata Lady Style showcases smooth, fluid movements, intricate footwork, and sensual hip motions, allowing dancers to express their femininity and grace on the dance floor. This style is typically performed to the same songs as traditional Bachata, but with a focus on individual expression and creativity. It requires good body control, technique, and a strong sense of rhythm and form. Bachata Lady Style is a captivating way for anyone to explore the beauty of Bachata as a solo dancer, and it offers a refreshing alternative for those looking to add a new dimension to their dance repertoire. Minimum age: 16

Participants will be part of a safe, positive and supportive environment, where everyone can feel free to learn, explore and discover new ways of moving through different contemporary dance styles. Classes will focus on studying specific movement vocabulary, correct alignment, improving coordination, memory and musicality. We will work with warm up sequences and centre work, some crossings to travel through the space and we will implement some improvisation, choreography and partnering. Dancers will study a fusion of contemporary dance styles, with a main focus on Release and Floorwork and a brief introduction to essential elements of Cunningham and Graham techniques. Creativity, physicality and openness are the key principles for this journey. Minimum age: 14

sessions.details: Instructor: Christina Mittelmaier | Level: All Levels | Room: Studio 3 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. CLASS DESCRIPTION: Do you want to limit your injury risk, while also building strength to become a better dancer? This fitness class will help you substantially improve your posture and correct any issues relating to your body alignment. It is very easy to create bad habits in ballet, so changing these habits often requires retraining muscle memory. Christina's Posture Power with PBT elements class will not only help you build up more strength and flexibility, but will also help you train the muscle memory required for automatically performing movements the right way. This class will be accompanied by music. With her experience as a coach, ballet teacher and approved PBT instructor, Christina has put together a class that will help you limit your injury risk and become a stronger and better dancer. Posture Power with PBT elements is a general level class and therefore suitable for those, who either wish to improve their ballet technique or simply improve their fitness level. It is also a perfect preparation for her 11:30 Int/Adv ballet class which takes place right after her Posture Power with PBT elements class. Progressing Ballet Technique (PBT) is an innovative programme which was developed by Marie Walton- Mahon in order to help dancers and students improve their technique. The programme includes carefully designed exercises focusing on training muscle memory and improving core strength, weight placement and alignment of the body. Minimum age: 14

sessions.title: Contemporary & Jazz - Graham Technique - Berenice Heger-Hedloy - BEG - £13
sessions.details: Instructor: Berenice Heger-Hedløy | Level: Beginner | Room: Studio 5 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com. CLASS DESCRIPTION This class will follow the traditional structure of the codified technique, sequencing through a set series of seated floor exercises, standing exercises and across-the-floor sequences. The Graham Technique is an immensely valuable technique to layer on top of any dance training as it emphasises the importance of the usage of the back, as well as accessing the deep core abdominal muscles.Fundamental principles in Graham Technique are the contraction and release, opposition, shift of weight and spirals. Graham dancers believe that it is important that we not simply see ourselves as objects in space when dancing, but that we expand their body, energy and focus so they are amplified enough to fill that space. The class will serve to free up the back, open up the hips and the body. On top, form the dancer to find dynamics, intricacy and articulation of the spine. Minimum age: 16

sessions.details: Instructor: Jovani Furlan | Level: Intermediate | Room: Studio 2 | Location: Danceworks | BIO:Jovani Furlan is a Principal Dancer with New York City Ballet. Born in Joinville, Brazil, he trained at the Bolshoi School in Brazil before joining Miami City Ballet, where he was promoted to Principal Dancer in 2017. He joined New York City Ballet in 2019 and has since performed leading roles in works by George Balanchine, Jerome Robbins, Justin Peck, Alexei Ratmansky, Christopher Wheeldon, and others. Alongside his performing career, Jovani is passionate about teaching and mentoring dancers, with a focus on musicality, clarity of technique, and individual artistry. CLASS DESCRIPTION: This class emphasizes musicality, coordination, and finding freedom within technique. Drawing from seven years of Vaganova training at the Bolshoi School and over fifteen years dancing professionally with Balanchine companies, the class explores how clear, grounded technique can be expanded and personalized. The guiding principle is that true freedom comes from proper technique. Dancers are supported in a positive, focused environment and leave with a sharper eye for their dancing, both as performers and audience members.

BIOBorn and raised in California, Tiffany began training at the San Francisco Ballet School at the age of 12. Her first professional engagement was with Boston Ballet II, followed by four seasons with Miami City Ballet. She then returned to Boston Ballet, where she was promoted to the rank of soloist. In 2014, Tiffany joined English National Ballet, also as a soloist, and remained with the company for ten and a half years—bringing her total professional career to an impressive 24 years.CLASS DESCRIPTIONThe class is geared for professionals, pre professionals and advanced students over 14 years of age. It is important to get a sense of what professional company class is like, so I feel it’s important to bring that atmosphere into the studio. CLASS DESCRIPTIONLA-style salsa is danced in a line where dancers break on the first beat of the music (on 1) rather than on 2. Many dancers find this timing more intuitive. LA-style is a fantastic starting point for those with or without prior dancing experience.The narrow path aspect of LA style makes it easy to keep track of where you are in and among all of those spins and turns you will very quickly be mastering. Minimum age: 16 This class is not suitable for absolute beginners.

CLASS DESCRIPTION: Do you want to limit your injury risk, while also building strength to become a better dancer? This fitness class will help you substantially improve your posture and correct any issues relating to your body alignment. It is very easy to create bad habits in ballet, so changing these habits often requires retraining muscle memory. Christina's Posture Power with PBT elements class will not only help you build up more strength and flexibility, but will also help you train the muscle memory required for automatically performing movements the right way. This class will be accompanied by music. With her experience as a coach, ballet teacher and approved PBT instructor, Christina has put together a class that will help you limit your injury risk and become a stronger and better dancer. Posture Power with PBT elements is a general level class and therefore suitable for those, who either wish to improve their ballet technique or simply improve their fitness level. It is also a perfect preparation for her 11:30 Int/Adv ballet class which takes place right after her Posture Power with PBT elements class. Progressing Ballet Technique (PBT) is an innovative programme which was developed by Marie Walton- Mahon in order to help dancers and students improve their technique. The programme includes carefully designed exercises focusing on training muscle memory and improving core strength, weight placement and alignment of the body. Minimum age: 14

sessions.details: Instructor: Jonathan Reid | Level: Beg | Room: Studio 5 | Location: In Studio Classes | Popping is one of the most amazing disciplines within the street dance world. Michael Jackson, Usher, Janet Jackson, Chris Brown, Ciara, Missy Elliot and Beyoncé are just a few names who have used this art form.No matter what your style, flavour or dance background, having popping within your vocabulary will take your dance/artistry to the next level and have you stand out from the rest.This class is a beginner/foundation class.What will it cover?• Popping foundation techniques and basics.• Basic in other styles used in popping (Waving, Tutting, Animation, Robot, Puppet Style, Gliding (Moonwalk), Mime, Boogaloo)• Control and high-level body isolation.• Tricks and blow up moves for battle, shows, auditions or just to show off on a night out.• Musicality (the different ways you can approach, play and connect with music)• Basic choreography, freestyle and how to get funky

sessions.title: Salsa on2 Groove & Technique
sessions.details: New to salsa, or curious to explore the groove and flow of salsa on2? This is the perfect place to start. You’ll build strong foundations in technique, partner connection while discovering how dance fits with the music. We offer 2 levels: Beginners: This class is designed to give you strong foundations from the very beginning. You’ll learn the groove, the stepping, and the connection to the music in a supportive and fun environment. No partner or prior experience needed. Transition & Foundations group: Already dancing salsa? Join this group if you want to feel more grounded in salsa on2. Expect footwork and partnerwork technique, body movement and plenty of groove! This class connects musicality training with salsa technique helping you really feel the music as you dance. 6-week course - every Tuesday at 7pm. London, Happy Feet Studios.

sessions.title: Salsa musicality jam
sessions.details: Each month we explore a different musicality theme in a progressive way — from diving into the role of individual instruments and the rhythms they create, to interpreting song structure, learning about salsa history and playing salsa percussion ourselves. You’ll learn how to connect to the music through both solo dancing and partnerwork. You will discover new ways to express salsa with groove, playfulness, and confidence. Sign up for a 6-week course. Not suitable for absolute beginners.
